title: Crypto-themed X.com auto-reply bot
description:
## Goal

Build a Node.js + TypeScript bot that ingests X.com mentions/replies via polling, generates crypto-themed responses with the OpenAI API, and posts replies back — with safety guardrails and idempotency.

## Architecture

Polling worker (X webhooks/AAA require Enterprise access, so we poll mentions on a schedule). A decoupled reply pipeline (dedupe -> filter -> OpenAI gen -> safety -> post -> persist) is reused by an optional AAA webhook receiver added later.

## Stack

- Node.js 20+, TypeScript
- twitter-api-v2 (X client)
- openai SDK (Chat/Responses + Moderation)
- Fastify (optional webhook + health)
- better-sqlite3 (since_id cursor + replied-ID dedupe)
- zod config, pino logging, node-cron/interval scheduler

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Constraints / Risks

- X Basic tier (~$200/mo) realistically required for reliable read+write.
- Automation/spam enforcement risk -> throttle + quality filters.
- Crypto + AI financial-advice liability -> moderation + disclaimer enforced in code.

title: Scaffold TS project + tooling
description:
## Context

Greenfield Node.js + TypeScript project for the X.com crypto bot. Establish the build/dev tooling so all later beads have a foundation.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] package.json with scripts: dev (tsx/ts-node watch), build (tsc), start, lint, format
- [ ] tsconfig.json (strict, ES2022, NodeNext)
- [ ] Deps installed: typescript, twitter-api-v2, openai, fastify, better-sqlite3, zod, dotenv, pino, node-cron
- [ ] Dev deps: tsx, eslint, prettier, @types/*
- [ ] .env.example with all required vars documented
- [ ] .gitignore (node_modules, dist, .env, *.db)
- [ ] src/index.ts placeholder builds and runs

## Notes

Use NodeNext module resolution. better-sqlite3 is native — note build prerequisites in README later.
close_reason: Approved by inspector. All 7 AC items satisfied; scope clean; no regressions. Guard confirmed lint/build/start/format all green. TS scaffold (package.json, tsconfig, eslint/prettier, .env.example, .gitignore, src/index.ts) ready as foundation for dependent beads.

comments.author: worker
comments.text:
[handoff worker→guard] Implementation complete. Scaffolded greenfield TS project on epic/xbot:
- package.json: scripts dev (tsx watch), build (tsc), start (node dist), lint (eslint), format (prettier)
- tsconfig.json: strict, ES2022, NodeNext module + moduleResolution
- Deps installed: typescript, twitter-api-v2, openai, fastify, better-sqlite3, zod, dotenv, pino, node-cron
- Dev deps: tsx, eslint, typescript-eslint, @eslint/js, prettier, @types/node, @types/better-sqlite3, @types/node-cron
- .env.example documents all required vars (X creds, OpenAI, bot behavior, DB path, server, logging)
- .gitignore: node_modules, dist, .env, *.db/*.sqlite
- src/index.ts placeholder

Verified locally: `npm install`, `npm run build` (clean), `npm run start` prints startup msg, `npm run lint` (clean), `npm run format` (clean). Ready for CI checks. Note: better-sqlite3 is native and compiled fine during install.

comments.author: guard
comments.text:
[handoff guard→inspector] All checks passed on epic/xbot:
- Lint (`npm run lint`): PASS — 0 errors, 0 warnings
- Build/Typecheck (`npm run build`, clean dist): PASS — tsc strict, no errors
- Start smoke (`npm run start`): PASS — prints startup message
- Format (`prettier --check`): PASS — all files conform

No test script configured (expected for greenfield scaffold). Ready for final review.

comments.author: technoch1ef
comments.text:
**Inspector judgment for bead bd-120.1**

### AC coverage
- [x] package.json scripts — dev (tsx watch), build (tsc), start, lint, format all present
- [x] tsconfig.json — strict, ES2022, NodeNext module + moduleResolution; extra safety flags
- [x] Deps installed — typescript, twitter-api-v2, openai, fastify, better-sqlite3, zod, dotenv, pino, node-cron
- [x] Dev deps — tsx, eslint, prettier, @types/node, @types/better-sqlite3, @types/node-cron, typescript-eslint, @eslint/js
- [x] .env.example — all required vars documented and grouped
- [x] .gitignore — node_modules, dist, .env, *.db (+ *.sqlite, *.db-journal, .DS_Store)
- [x] src/index.ts placeholder — builds and runs (guard-confirmed)

### Diff scope
OK: all changed files in scope. Beyond source/config, only package-lock.json (npm install) and .beads/* (tracking) — both expected.

### Regression sniff
OK: no risky patterns. console.log in src/index.ts is an intentional placeholder; structured logging via pino deferred to a later bead per Notes.

### Skill review
OK: village-workflow conventions followed — work on epic/xbot, local commit, clean handoff chain.

### Good Practices
- Strict tsconfig exceeds minimum (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, noImplicitOverride, noFallthroughCasesInSwitch).
- .env.example is thorough and self-documenting with sensible defaults.
- Flat eslint config with typescript-eslint recommended, ignoring dist/node_modules.

**Verdict: approve**

title: Optional AAA webhook receiver
description:
## Context

Feature-flagged Fastify endpoint for Account Activity API push events, reusing the same pipeline. Off by default (Enterprise-only).

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/server.ts Fastify app with /health
- [ ] src/x/webhook.ts handles AAA CRC challenge (HMAC-SHA256) on GET
- [ ] POST event route normalizes mentions and feeds pipeline
- [ ] Enabled only when WEBHOOK_ENABLED=true
- [ ] Signature verification of inbound events

## Notes

Decoupled so AAA can be enabled later without touching core logic.

title: Entrypoint + graceful shutdown
description:
## Context

Wire everything together and handle lifecycle.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/index.ts loads config, inits store, starts poller
- [ ] Conditionally starts Fastify server when WEBHOOK_ENABLED=true
- [ ] SIGINT/SIGTERM graceful shutdown (stop poller, close DB/server)
- [ ] Single pino logger instance shared

## Notes

Health endpoint should report poller liveness.

title: Dockerfile + deployment
description:
## Context

Containerize the long-running worker for VPS/Fly/Railway deployment.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Multi-stage Dockerfile (build deps for better-sqlite3, slim runtime)
- [ ] Non-root user; persistent volume for SQLite DB
- [ ] HEALTHCHECK against /health (or process check)
- [ ] Honors env-based config; no secrets baked in
- [ ] Image builds and container boots in DRY_RUN

## Notes

Document required native build packages.

title: README + persona prompt + .env docs
description:
## Context

Operator docs: setup, X API tier requirements, configuration, running, and the default crypto persona.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] prompts/persona.md default crypto persona (no financial advice)
- [ ] README: setup, full env var table, X Basic-tier requirement + rate-limit caveats
- [ ] Run instructions: local dev, DRY_RUN, Docker
- [ ] Automation/spam-policy + financial-advice disclaimer notes
- [ ] Troubleshooting (better-sqlite3 native build, auth errors)

## Notes

Call out that true webhooks need Enterprise AAA; polling is the default path.

title: Config loader with zod-validated env
description:
## Context

Central typed config validated at startup so the app fails fast on missing/invalid env.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/config.ts exports a validated, typed config object (zod)
- [ ] Validates X creds: X_APP_KEY, X_APP_SECRET, X_ACCESS_TOKEN, X_ACCESS_SECRET, BOT_USER_ID
- [ ] Validates OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_MODEL (default gpt-4o-mini), OPENAI_TEMPERATURE
- [ ] Validates POLL_INTERVAL_SEC, MAX_REPLIES_PER_RUN, DRY_RUN (bool), PERSONA_PROMPT or PERSONA_PROMPT_PATH
- [ ] Throws a clear aggregated error listing all missing/invalid vars
- [ ] No secrets logged

## Notes

Load .env via dotenv only outside production; allow real env in prod.

comments.author: worker
comments.text:
[handoff worker→guard] Implementation complete. Ready for CI checks.

Added src/config.ts: zod-validated, typed `config` object loaded at import time (fail-fast).
- X creds: X_APP_KEY, X_APP_SECRET, X_ACCESS_TOKEN, X_ACCESS_SECRET, BOT_USER_ID (all required, non-empty)
- OPENAI_API_KEY (required), OPENAI_MODEL (default gpt-4o-mini), OPENAI_TEMPERATURE (0–2, default 0.7)
- POLL_INTERVAL_SEC (default 300), MAX_REPLIES_PER_RUN (default 5), DRY_RUN (bool coercion), PERSONA_PROMPT or PERSONA_PROMPT_PATH (at least one required via superRefine)
- loadConfig() throws a single aggregated Error listing every invalid/missing var by name + reason; no secret values logged or embedded in messages
- dotenv loaded only when NODE_ENV !== production

Verified locally: prettier clean, eslint clean, `tsc --noEmit` passes. Did not run tests (guard's job).

title: SQLite store: cursor + reply dedupe
description:
## Context

Persistence for idempotency: track the since_id cursor and the set of source tweet IDs already replied to, surviving restarts/crashes.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/store/db.ts initializes better-sqlite3 with schema (migrations on boot)
- [ ] getCursor()/setCursor(sinceId) for mentions pagination
- [ ] hasReplied(tweetId) / markReplied(tweetId) idempotency helpers
- [ ] DB path configurable; created if absent
- [ ] Prepared statements; no SQL injection

## Notes

Single-writer assumption (one worker). Keep schema minimal.

title: X API client wrapper + rate-limit handling
description:
## Context

Thin wrapper over twitter-api-v2 providing the only X operations the bot needs, with backoff for 429s and rate-limit awareness.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/x/client.ts authenticates with OAuth 1.0a user context (post) using configured creds
- [ ] getMentions(sinceId?) returns new mentions with author + text + conversation context
- [ ] reply(text, inReplyToTweetId) posts a reply
- [ ] Handles 429 with exponential backoff honoring rate-limit reset headers
- [ ] Typed return shapes; errors surfaced, not swallowed

## Notes

Use expansions/fields to fetch author username + referenced tweet for context.

title: OpenAI crypto reply generation
description:
## Context

Generate a crypto-themed reply from an incoming mention using the OpenAI SDK with a configurable persona system prompt.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/ai/generate.ts builds messages from persona system prompt + incoming tweet (+ limited thread context)
- [ ] Persona loaded from PERSONA_PROMPT or PERSONA_PROMPT_PATH (default in prompts/persona.md)
- [ ] Output constrained to <=280 chars (instruction + hard truncation fallback)
- [ ] Model/temperature from config
- [ ] Returns generated text + token usage for logging

## Notes

Default persona: knowledgeable, witty crypto-native voice. Never gives financial advice (enforced separately in safety layer).

title: Safety guardrails (moderation + disclaimer)
description:
## Context

Enforce safety on generated replies regardless of persona prompt: financial-advice liability and content moderation.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/pipeline/safety.ts runs OpenAI Moderation on generated text; block on flag
- [ ] Strips/blocks explicit financial-advice phrasing; appends short disclaimer when needed
- [ ] Hard char-limit enforcement (<=280) after any modification
- [ ] Profanity/spam guard
- [ ] Returns { ok, text, reason? }

## Notes

Guardrails live in code, not just the prompt, so they cannot be bypassed by persona config.

title: Inbound mention filter
description:
## Context

Decide which mentions deserve a reply before spending OpenAI tokens.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/pipeline/filter.ts skips the bot's own tweets and retweets
- [ ] Skips already-replied IDs (via store)
- [ ] Optional blocklist of user IDs/handles from config
- [ ] Optional language filter
- [ ] Pure/testable predicate returning a skip reason

## Notes

Keep filtering cheap and side-effect free.

title: Reply pipeline orchestrator
description:
## Context

Orchestrate per-tweet processing, reused by both poller and optional webhook ingestion.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/pipeline/process.ts: filter -> generate -> safety -> post -> markReplied
- [ ] Respects DRY_RUN (log intended reply, do not post)
- [ ] Respects MAX_REPLIES_PER_RUN throttle
- [ ] Structured pino logging per stage with tweet IDs + token usage
- [ ] Errors on one tweet do not abort the batch

## Notes

Ingestion-agnostic: accepts a normalized mention object.

title: Polling worker (scheduler)
description:
## Context

Primary ingestion: periodically fetch new mentions and feed the pipeline, advancing the cursor.

## Skills

- village-workflow

## Branch

`epic/xbot`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] src/x/poller.ts loops on POLL_INTERVAL_SEC (interval or node-cron)
- [ ] Fetches mentions since stored cursor, processes via pipeline
- [ ] Advances cursor only after successful batch handling
- [ ] Backoff on rate-limit; never overlapping runs
- [ ] Graceful start/stop

## Notes

Guard against overlapping ticks if a run exceeds the interval.
